title: ranunculus flabellatus subsp. chaerophylloides
intro: Ranunculus flabellatus subsp. chaerophylloides, also known as the yellow water buttercup, is a plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or consumed orally, although its use should be cautious due to potential toxicity.
